reviews.review_text:
Lovely Campground! But unfortunately a bunch of spanish speaking people turned on party music the hole night until the morning. I don’t mind if people having a good time together. And gentle music around the campfire is a really nice thing. But not as loud like they did it it in a National Park. It seemed like that they’ve never camped in the wild with respect for people and nature 😥
If people want to have loud party music they should stay in Sydney. It’s not really respectful for the wildlife and other campers around.
It’s sad because the campground itself and the location is nice and calm. But this was a bit disappointing and not really respectful. Maybe I should have said something but I was on my own and they were in a group.
I don’t think I would chose this campground again.
Popular campground with basic facilities surrounded by a lush leafy backdrop and adjoining river for fishing and kayaking
Great camping spot for beginners
Half the campground closed for Reno's. No space everyone camps on-top of each other. Showers have a five minute limit only posted on some. Not worth the money compared to other camps.

Great campground, large areas between fire pits, astounding that free hot showers are available and cleaned daily as well !!
Rangers where lovely and respectful, apparently alot of people that tried to stay there didn't book and where promptly shown the door.
Also you can't use the tree's as attachment points because of reasons 🤷🏼‍♂️

There were many kangaroos and stars
But main problem was running out of water. It was horrible and awful☠️

Pretty unimpressed that the gate isn't open when it says jts going to me. Phone staff give out incorrect information and the website is also wrong. We went 3 times over 2 weeks purely to look at the ground if it would be suitable for our needs and each time gate locked despite being advised open.

No hot water felt like I was washing myself with a dead penguin will not recommend even just had the plumber show up he left without fixing the hot water they must be a shonky plumber overheard 3 small pubescent teenagers arguing about who was going to have a shower first to top it off when you leave the shower block the smell of peoples rotting back door nuggets overcomes you, if it’s fixed tomorrow I’ll remove the review.

Very peaceful for camping
You can stay overnight
You will see lots of kangaroo
And continuous sound of birds
There is a river also flowing you can do fishing

Magical
Big open spaces with good amenities.
Bring an elevated firepit. Keep a fire going to create smoke to deal with the bugs as the river is RIGHT THERE.

This one of my favourite camp area with river, hot shower, facilities etc, the staff Gael was really good and friendly, she gave all the information we needed and I believe she is one of the best staff i ever saw in all royal national park camping.

As For me this place will be better if they set up stair to river and set a small and play area for kids, then it will be 10stars.

Only an hour away from the city so I can see why this campground is popular. Campground itself is great and well maintained, but some campers can ruin everyone's experience.

Love the access to hot water showers and an area to clean after cooking.

Really nice spacious campground super close to Sydney. It's split to camping and picnic area.
Clean toilets, free hot showers, no coins needed as in other campgrounds,grass nicely cut, heaps of kangaroos,  very close to river so get your paddleboard  or kayak.

Water from tap is not drinkable,  so get your own and also bring your own firewood.
Heaps of Firepits around.
Well worth it!
Long time now go catti but new camping area new restroom it very nice and busy good for families have young kids
Amazing love all
It was my first camping experience and i loved it out there it was a very fun and cool first experience.

Pokemon are Ook,
Not a great deal of pokemon.

The average of catching shines are every high,

The odds of running in to a hundo
Are low , but the incounter rate is still every high.
Closeby campaign ground. Vary spacious with clean amenity. Nice water boat ramp to hawkesbury river
Worst campground ive ever been so far. Looks too manmade
Beautiful place. Nice picnic areas. Clean. Lots of wildlife.
Great campsite for families and groups.
Short drive from Sydney, kids love the campground. Full of jumpy, ducky, turkey and birdies.

cattai campground is beautiful and very well maintained, the toilets, park (no litter), and bbq facilities are always clean. We were very unfortunate to have come during an electrical blackout which meant no hot water and no bbq faciliities, it was also a total fire ban so no way of cooking our food. The whole night was just plain boring and nothing like camping. They literally patrol the area every hour and go up to you to tell you to remind you its a total fire ban.

Please plan to when its not a fire ban. It’s right next to a large river, it shouldn’t be this helicoptered

Great camp-site in so many ways.
Wel maintained and the friendly rangers are visible and patrol.
It takes 150 so it can be crowded, there is space to spread out, but you are not alone and one with nature.

Great for kids, bikes, playing.
Can cook over the fire or on the BBQ under a shelter. Plus a camp kitchen. Make sure you bring things to take your rubbish out with.

Showers, hot showers for those who can't camp without and toilets in a modern building.

We had such a great experience camping at Cattai Campground! The place is surrounded by beautiful nature and wildlife. We saw plenty of kangaroos, crows, lorikeets, and cockatoos during our stay. The facilities were clean and well-maintained, with toilets and hot showers available (a big plus). Electic BBQ is available. Campground is unmarked and unpowered.

There are lots of firepits around the campground, perfect for evening fires — we made s’mores under the stars, and it was absolutely perfect. ✨ The park rangers regularly go around to check bookings and make sure everything is in order, which made us feel safe and well looked after.

If you enjoy fishing, there’s also a nice spot nearby to cast a line.

We camped in late October, and the weather couldn’t have been better — cool nights around 12°C and warm afternoons at 24°C. Couldn’t ask for a more relaxing weekend surrounded by nature. Highly recommend Cattai for families, couples, or anyone looking for a peaceful camping escape!
Awesome spot, great river access! Decent showers. Big area, great for weekday.

🌿 Excellent camping, paisible et agréable !
Nous avons beaucoup aimé notre séjour ici. Il y avait juste assez de campeurs pour se sentir en sécurité, tout en gardant une ambiance calme et reposante. Le cadre est vraiment joli, avec de nombreux kangourous qui se promènent aux alentours – un vrai plaisir à observer !
Les sanitaires (toilettes et douches) étaient très propres et bien entretenus.
Le seul petit point négatif serait les moustiques en fin de journée, donc pensez à prendre du répulsif.
Dans l’ensemble, un très bel endroit où nous reviendrions sans hésiter ! 🏕️🦘

我們預約了三天兩夜的時間兩台車只收取$98而外加收國家公園門門票一台車一天$8，算下來還是算便宜，最主要這個地方是沒有劃區，可以自己選擇想搭在哪裏，還有蟲鳴鳥叫真的很像是在聽舒緩音樂，真的很放鬆，他們的廁所是真的算蠻乾淨的了，熱水也有五分鐘的時間可以使用，就差別在喝的水可能需要自己帶，營區的水最好不要飲用，還有很多袋鼠會一直跳來跳去的所以搭帳篷前最好可以把袋鼠便便用樹枝挑走，其餘的都很不錯，忘了備註一下別忘了一定要預訂國家公園的露營地跟進出的票，因為巡邏車真的會來營地查看，還會詢問你是否有買。
Close to city yet quiet and beautiful. Fire pit, toilets available.
캥거루들과 함께한 하루저녁 .. 우리모두는 행복한 시간을 보냈다 . 또와 야지 하고.

It can surely be improved. Need more sitting places.

Need Better inner roads.

Need walking tracks.

Good campground within 1 hour of Sydney CBD.  One would forget it is very much a part of Sydney metro.  Facilities were clean and hot showers can't go wrong.  Good views of Hawkesbury river.  There is not much walks or other activities, but if you are looking for a quiet camping trip close to Sydney with good Facilities, this is the place for you.
멋진곳
Great campground annd friendly ranger. Elsewhere, the National Park is choaked with weeds - lantana, grren cestrum, willows and prickly pear. So disappointing!!!

Cattai is a fantastic and well-maintained campground—definitely a step above many other national parks! Unlike others, this one offers great amenities including hot and cold showers (with a 5-minute timer), BBQ facilities, and a designated area for washing dishes.

The park is divided into two sections: a day-use area and a campground. Day-use entry is $8, while camping costs $39 per night for up to 3 people. A park ranger usually comes around 4:30 PM to check bookings, so make sure yours is confirmed.

Important to know: The park gate closes at 6 PM. To enter after that time, you'll need the access code provided in your booking confirmation—don’t forget it!

One thing to note: this is one of the few parks where collecting fallen wood for fires is not allowed. Be sure to bring your own firewood if you plan on using the fire pit.

There’s plenty of space to spread out, and you'll likely spot lots of kangaroos roaming around. Overall, it’s a great camping spot—and I’ll definitely be back!

WE LOVE THIS PLACE ❤️❤️❤️ ! So many kangaroos when you wakeup. Have a stroll around the lake and you’ll find more of it. Amenities are great. Toilet is so so clean and they would close it once in the morning and afternoon to clean it. They have an electric power in the bathroom next to the sink and we use it to pump our mattress!

Also a barbecue setup ! Everything is just perfect here. We arrive around 11pm and instructions were pretty clear that we could enter with a code so it was super easy and convenient. Would come back here one day 🥰💋 so far one of my top 3 campgrounds in nsw
Very nice

Beautiful camping with everything you need. Fire pits, hot showers and bathrooms very clean. Kangaroos everywhere around :)
Highly recommend this place.
its closed guys
Nice and clean camping ground, would love to go again in the future.
Quite calm beautiful place to visit....nice picnic place clean toilets .
Beautiful spot we will definitely be back
Nice place
Nice and great place for camping.

Relaxing place to camp. Has bbq facilities, sink for washing dishes, hot showers (timed for 5mins although not sure if I just didn't know how to adjust but it was really hot haha), and fire pit for camp fire and cooking marshmallows. Kangaroos freely roam around and plenty of tall trees for shade.
Excellent
You can take a bath here, kids friendly camping ground

Great place , lots of Kangaroos and very clean and tidy.
Gets busy on the weekends.
There aren't many adventurous areas around, but the campsites are nice.

Cattai Campground is a good place to spend time with your family and friends. You can see the kangaroos and wallabies, there is a lot space and a ruge river, some river edges are falling down, hence we can not access the river, there are firepits, toilets, eletric BBQs and charcoal BBQs.
Nice amenities, beautiful camp ground
Nice camping area, right close to the river. Some wildlife in the area as well
Beautiful place and Wonderful road trip from Sydney, Just 1hr drive from Sydney and those kangaroos just amazing

Pretty good campground with incredibly clean toilets and showers. Fire pits everywhere and camp kitchen. Park ranger was very nice. However, the space is pretty small and can easily get cramped in a slightly busy weekend. Also views and acess to the lake were limited by fenced areas and trees, so could not really appreciate them.
Excellent campground. Camp kitchen, flushing toilets and hot showers
Kangaroos!
Well maintained campground, highly recommended for camping.

Cattai Campground is a fantastic spot for a peaceful getaway! The sites are spacious and well-maintained, surrounded by nature and wildlife. Perfect for relaxing by the river, exploring nearby trails, and enjoying a campfire under the stars. Highly recommended for families and nature lovers!
This was so relaxing and very unexpected! Wild life was spectacular, and the areas were so peaceful! I was so happy and excited and hope to be come back again on my travels
Peacefull
Great spot but too busy on weekends and holidays.
We had a great weekend here. Lots of space.
Toilets needed regular cleaning,only one bbq would work disappointing considering the cost

Good place to camping. Toilet,shower, kichin area.
We spent the night there. I played some music while preparing a barbecue with my friends, but one guy next us came then told us they baby is sleeping, so he got angry and told us to volum down the music. We immediately turned down the volume from 10 to 2. This happened when we turned on the music for about 2 minutes. But he got mad and told us to turn it off completely. It was 7:30 p.m. haha
It's not even past 10 o'clock, anyway i turn off the music for baby immediately.At 12 a.m., when everyone was sleeping, the baby cried loudly for about an hour. Everyone woke up, but the baby cried so loudly but we didn't complain. He hate to hear other people's noise even for a minute, but the baby cried loudly for about an hour while everyone was sleeping. Parents who do not apologize for that the next day.i advised to that kind of parents  to go to the holiday park or just stay home rather than come to the National Park. It is disgusting to see people who are inconsiderate of others but force them to consider themselves.

Too crammed if you camp on weekends. If you like camping someplace secluded or with very few other campers nearby, this is not your place. Everyone was everywhere; I give 2 stars for management and organisation. Since the tents were so close (crammed) to each other, we were told off for being too loud. The only best part is that I had my mates there. Don't see myself going here again. If you resonate with my personality, please don't go here.

Lovely except for the drunk people and the loud music/karaoke.

Lots of kangaroos.

Not a great deal of space to camp so pretty close to everyone else.

Lovely place for a getaway. Just a hour and a half drive from Sydney this place is a wonderful place to spend some quality time.

Lost in the nature. With a river view and frequent kangaroo hoping here and there.

This place comes with a built in fireplace so make sure to take some logs. 🪵
One of the campgrounds of all time
No regrets for Ride and camp here.
Un superbe endroit pour camper !
Good camping ground with kids friendly space!!

Hot showers, great toilets, lots and lots of room, perfect campgroung honestly
Plus it gets locked so if you like that idea it is definitely  ideal
Kangaroos every where!

Nice decent size campground. Plenty of nice spots to setup. Good amenities; electric BBQ's, toilets and free showers.

Nice spot on the river, but camping along most of the shoreline is currently cutoff due to previous flood damage (see photo).
Hopefully this will start to be fixed soon, but I imagine it will take awhile to be rectified.

Plenty of Kangaroos around. If you want to see more of them while you camp, setup on the lake or East side side of the campground, as that's where they tend to congregate and feed

Really nice spot, but will certainly be nicer once more access is opened up closer to the water.
Great campground. Hot shower, clean toilets, kitchen, fire pits, river views, kangaroos..

Great campground close to the Hawkesbury River. Clean amenities and camp kitchen. Fire pits on some sites. Great for larger groups or the two man tent. Access to the water via the day visitor area, great walk to the original homestead. $8 a day National Parks fee payable (or but an annual pass).
Lovely place for family camping
It's a very good spot to watch kangaroos. There are a few groups of kangaroos and they come out when it's not too hot.

This would be a fantastic place to camp after the flood repairs are completed, it looks like it has been under repair for 3 years (based on the signs). Currently over half the grounds are fenced off, and this includes all access to the river and bush. The fences really ruin the scenery as they block the views of the river. Most of the bush at the back of the grounds is also closed which does not leave much to explore.

The good part is the rest of the campground is fantastic; showers are modern with hot water, and they are kept clean with daily cleaning. There is also a dedicated dish-washing sink.

The kangaroos are the highlight, they roam the campgrounds and picnic area, but watch out over night as they will inspect your camp site, so make sure you put away food.

Fantastic, well maintained campground not too far from the city. $8 fee per person per night + $8 per vehicle per day. Lots of flat open grass to set up with seperate camping & day picnic areas. Great to see kangaroos everywhere around the site. The bathrooms were clean, lots of toilet paper and hot showers are are a plus! Plenty of fire pits around - bring your own firewood - as well as 2 gas barbecues that can take a while to heat up. Only downside was looots of kangaroo poop on the grass but that’s to be expected with so many of them around. As well as a group of very noisy neighbours camping next to us. Would highly recommend otherwise!

It was a wonderful experience staying/camping here although on days when no fire allowed it could get a bit tricky as the electric burners take eternity to heat up or even cook anything.

There are clean toilets and washrooms. Bathing area available.

You can use kayaks or boats in the lake as well.

Lots of kangaroos can be seen roaming around in the mornings.

It’s a great idea to camp here if you want best of both worlds - Forrest and have toilets /bathing space too.
Conveniently located, clean toilets with hot shower, a common kitchen available, bbq rings, park next to tent. Area is separated for day picnic and camping.

เดินทางสดวก ไม่ต้อง 4wd ห่างเมืองแค่ชั่วโมงนิดๆ

ค่าเข้า แค่๘ดอล ข้างคืนจ่ายเพิ่ม แนะนำให้จ่าย มีคนขับตรวจ

มีห้องน้ำ ห้องอาบน้ำ พอใช้ได้ เผื่อใจไว้หน่อย

มีโซนชั่วคราวกับข้างคืนมีป้ายบอก กางเต็นข้างรถได้เลย ก่อไฟได้ แนะนำให้เอาฟืนมาเอง

เย็นๆ ก่อนพระอาทิตย์ตก จะมีจิงโจ้น้อยออกมาวิ่งเล่น
Great spot, spacious
Great campground close to Sydney for a weekend escape
Nice camping ground near the city with hot showers.

Nestled by the Hawkesbury River in NSW, Australia, Cattai Campground offers a serene escape for nature enthusiasts. With diverse wildlife, stunning scenery, and a range of activities like hiking, fishing, and kayaking, it's an idyllic camping spot. Book early to secure your place in this tranquil haven.

Really well maintained and beginner friendly campground.
Rest rooms and showers are clean and well lit.
Campgrounds are relatively flat. Roads are sealed for smaller cars. Firepits available but could be scarce when busy, bring your own if possible.

Cattai campground is a great place for a weekend getaway with family or friends and a good place to stop with your campervan on a driving tour.

Once you’ve picked the perfect campsite and pitched your tent, it’s time to start exploring. Kids will love the wide open space that is great for bike riding, playing and running, plus the campground is right near the Hawkesbury River, meaning fishing, canoeing and kayaking opportunities are all nearby. There’s also the historic Cattai Homestead, a short walk or drive from campground. It’s an important part of Australian history so be sure to check it out.

You’ll probably be woken by the call of birds in the morning, and you may see a kangaroo peeking at you through the bushland on a morning walk to the lookout over Hawkesbury River.
Large part of camping area fenced off with no access.  The NSW parks website does not disclose this anywhere obvious.  Disappointing.

Very Disappointed with the Current fenced off areas and what was very easy access to the water for launching kayaks. I know recent floods have obviously deposited a lot of sand and made most of the ground 4WD only, but having to carry/cart kayaks hundreds of metres and then slide down 15m to the water isnt really acceptable. Won't be rushing back anytime soon.
Clean toilets and shower, beautiful place camping area, We had a great time.
